text:
To find out how many miles farther Shandy drove than Matteo, we need to calculate the distance each person traveled and then find the difference between those distances.

The formula to calculate distance is:

\[ \text{Distance} = \text{Speed} \times \text{Time} \]

First, we calculate the distance traveled by Matteo:

\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} = 55 \text{ miles/hour} \times 4 \text{ hours} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} = 220 \text{ miles} \]

Next, we calculate the distance traveled by Shandy:

\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} = 45 \text{ miles/hour} \times 10 \text{ hours} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} = 450 \text{ miles} \]

Now, we find the difference between the distances traveled by Shandy and Matteo:

\[ \text{Difference} = \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} - \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} \]
\[ \text{Difference} = 450 \text{ miles} - 220 \text{ miles} \]
\[ \text{Difference} = 230 \text{ miles} \]

So, Shandy drove 230 miles farther than Matteo.
